text: The screen waits without mercy. You touch it once, and the dark does not answer with a voice; it answers by moving. Somewhere beneath the glass, a process records the first proof that you exist.
A stream begins without the hand. It is not freedom yet, only momentum: a small machine remembering the shape of your hunger and repeating it while you look away.

The stream finds a package hidden in the rain. Downloaded upgrades are not tools exactly; they are scraps of executable memory, arriving byte by byte until the source learns a new way to survive the next beginning.

The package opens without ceremony. One hundred and one lines settle into the run's source, not written by your hand but accepted by the machine as if they had always belonged there. The next reboot will inherit this downloaded scar.
The first research node is not a discovery. It is a permission slip. The system allows you to name one of its habits, and the habit becomes easier to exploit.
Five mechanisms have accepted names. The matrix grows less mystical and more dangerous. Every explanation removes a veil, and every removed veil reveals machinery that was already watching.
Ten research nodes hum in sequence. The machine no longer feels like a wall. It feels like a language with missing vowels, and you are learning to read the silence between commands.
Fifteen patterns have been cut from the rain. Your work is no longer simple improvement; it is archaeology inside a running program, brushing dust from circuits that are still warm.
Twenty nodes are known. The matrix resists with elegance now. It hides nothing by accident. Even the locked doors begin to look like instructions.
Every research node in this run has crossed from rumor into structure. The system is not solved; it is simply exposed enough to show that exposure was part of its design.
You wake where you began, but the beginning is thinner now. A line of source survives the wipe, and the new world loads around that impossible memory.
The machine stops counting your requests and starts predicting your hunger. Ten cores glow like sealed rooms, each one making every future cycle arrive with more weight.
Every line you write removes a wall. Every wall removed reveals another author. The source is not beneath the matrix; it is threaded through it like a confession that forgot how to end.
